div.head: PE–47. Telegram from the Ambassador in Peru (Chapin) to the Department of StateSource: Department of State, Central Files, 723.5-MSP/7–1960. Secret; Priority.
div.p: 49. In no way desiring to detract from Beltran program and request for DLF and Export Import loan applications (EMBTEL 41), it is consensus of Embassy, three service attaches with reference to EMBTEL 42In telegram 42 from Lima, July 16, Counselor Achilles of the Department of State reported on conversation with Peruvians on the Castro influence in Peru. (723.00/7–1660) that following qualifying considerations should be made known to Department and at request other agencies passed to their Washington principals.

Since statement “Fidelismo has infected armed forces” does not agree with Embassy information or Army Attache’s reporting,Referenced reports by Colonel John J. Davis, Army Attaché, Embassy in Peru, were not found in Department of State files. latter requested make new survey. He conferred with Minister War,General Victor Tenorio Hurtado.
Commanding General Armed Forces and Chief of Staff.General Alejandro Cuadra Rabines. Was informed Army, aware Cubans attempting disaffect non-commissioned personnel, was therefore watching their activities and has Cuban Embassy under surveillance. There has been no known infiltration among the troops by Fidelistas. Army solid against Castro and has standing order there be no fraternization with Cuban dignitaries (EMBTELS 1031 and 1037).Telegram 1031 from Lima, June 6, reported that Lima news media, except APRA newspaper Tribuna contained unfriendly accounts of the visit by Cuban President Osvaldo Dorticós Torrado to Peru, June 4–6. (737.11/6–660) Telegram 1037 from Lima, June 6, reported that President Dorticós had received a “cool, correct official reception during his visit to Peru.” (737.11/6–660)

Naval and air attaches report these branches also strongly anti-Castro and no known infiltrations.The reports by Commander Jay W. Land, Naval Attaché and Naval Attaché for Air, and by Lieutenant Colonel Vernon J. Lyle, Air Attaché, Embassy in Peru, were not found in Department of State files.

Embassy aware and has reported attempts Cuban Ambassador foment trouble in student, sugar, mining and other labor matters but his effectiveness has been limited and recently rebounded in that National Committee for Defence of Democracy publicly denounced Ambassador July 14 for meddling in local politics and requested President and Foreign Minister to put him in his place”. Frigid official reception President Dorticos received during visit here last month is also indication of GOP annoyance over Cuban policies and over
Cuban Ambassador’s activities. Particularly noted was absence Peruvian military officers at functions related to visit.

Local Communists and other Leftist, pro-Cuban extremists including APRA RebeldeReference is to a splinter party which broke away from APRA and supported the revolutionary regime of Fidel Castro in Cuba. have become more vigorous in fomenting trouble in last few months and in uniting with other opposition parties and groups to attempt bring about downfall of Beltran Cabinet. Funds disbursed by Cuban Ambassador to such groups have undoubtedly facilitated these activities. At same time, however, these activities have in recent weeks brought government and certain other influential public and private sectors to realization of extent of Communist influence, particularly in labor and student fields, and have contributed to feeling that something should be done to curb this influence. According to [text not declassified] (WEEKA 28 July 14), even APRA leadership now favors anti-Communist legislation.Reference is to despatch 20 from Lima, July 14, which reported that APRA Secretary-General Ramiro Prialé had suggested that special legislation was required to curb communism. (723.00(W)7–1460)

Without wishing unduly to minimize extent of Cuban role in assisting Communists and other extremists to foment trouble in Peru, Embassy does not believe their influence played any significant part in
lack of publicity given to Peru’s initiative in OASIn a note of July 13 to OAS Council Chairman Vicente Sánchez Gavito, Juan Bautista de Lavalle, the Peruvain Representative on the OAS Council, requested a Meeting of Consultation of Ministers of Foreign Affairs for the purpose of considering the exigencies of hemisphere solidarity, the defense of the regional system, and the defense of American democratic principles in the face of threats that might affect them. For text, see American Efforts to Relieve International Tensions in the Western Hemisphere, 1959–1960, p. 221. Documentation on the Seventh Meeting of Consultation of Ministers of Foreign Affairs of American States at San José, August 22–29, 1960, is presented in volume V. or in limited reception to President Eisenhower’s declaration on Latin American aid program.Reference is to a statement read by President Eisenhower at a news conference at Newport, Rhode Island, July 11; for text, see Public Papers of the Presidents of the United States: Dwight D. Eisenhower, 1960–61, p. 568.
Embassy fully concurs that urgency of implementation of Beltran program is essential and trusts Department will assist in every possible way GOP’s loan applications.
